labels.shortDescription: Error toast shown when copying to the clipboard fails
labels.value: Couldn't copy to the clipboard. Try again or copy the value manually.

labels.shortDescription: Cross-link action to open the correlated logs in the Log Console
Modal cancel button label
Class Name input label
Class Name pattern validation error
labels.value: Class name must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.

labels.shortDescription: Class Name input placeholder
Class Name required validation error
Code container language label (shown above each code block)
Inline error shown in the modal when copying the generated code fails
labels.value: Couldn't copy to the clipboard. Select the code and copy it manually, or use the download button.

labels.shortDescription: Assistive text for the copy-resolver button
Assistive text for the copy-test button
Modal done button label
Assistive text for the download-resolver button
Assistive text for the download-test button
Class Type Resolver Setup modal purpose sentence
labels.value: Kern lives in its own managed-package namespace, so it can only find subscriber classes declared as global. This resolver lets Kern locate your regular public classes (trigger handlers, validation rules, scheduled jobs, and web services) without forcing you to declare every one of them as global.

labels.shortDescription: Resolver class tab label
Setup step 1 — copy the resolver class
labels.value: Copy the Resolver Class code from the tab below.

labels.shortDescription: Setup step 2 — create the Apex class via Setup
labels.value: Create the Apex class: go to Setup > Apex Classes > New, paste the code, and click Save. Repeat for the Test Class tab. (Developers can also deploy via SFDX, VS Code, or Workbench if preferred.)

labels.shortDescription: Setup step 3 — register the resolver via Custom Metadata
labels.value: Register the resolver: go to Setup > Custom Metadata Types > Class Type Resolver > Manage Records, then either edit the existing record or click New. Set Class Name to {0} and save.

labels.shortDescription: Setup step 4 — confirmation that Kern picks up the resolver automatically
labels.value: Done. Kern now uses your resolver automatically to locate your custom triggers, validation rules, scheduled jobs, and web service handlers.

labels.shortDescription: Assistive text for the setup steps icon
Setup Steps callout heading
Test class tab label
Class Type Resolver Setup modal header title
Masking advisor card title.
Coverage advisor empty-state body, no object selected.
labels.value: Choose an object above to see which of its fields would benefit from a masking rule on framework paths.

labels.shortDescription: Coverage advisor empty-state heading.
describeFields failure error message.
labels.value: Could not load fields for this object. Try again, or pick a different object.

labels.shortDescription: Coverage advisor section heading.
Heuristic-only mode informational notice.
labels.value: Running in heuristic-only mode — native field classification could not be read in this org. Matches below are flagged by field name and type, not by your classification metadata.

labels.shortDescription: Heuristic confidence tier label: HIGH.
Heuristic confidence tier label: LOW.
Heuristic confidence tier label: MEDIUM.
Manual-review row note: no rule matches the field.
Other-fields section heading with count.
Other-fields section explanatory copy.
labels.value: Fields not recommended for masking. Likely misses — where the field type suggests sensitive data but no rule matched — are listed first. Use Review to mask one anyway.

labels.shortDescription: Ready section guidance copy.
labels.value: These fields match a masking rule and are pre-selected. Uncheck any you want to leave out, then export the package.

labels.shortDescription: Coverage table column header: Field.
Field table column header: Sensitivity.
Dead-config banner heading.
Dead-config banner summary, plural. {0}=count.
labels.value: {0} active masking targets are currently masking nothing.

labels.shortDescription: Dead-config banner summary, singular.
labels.value: 1 active masking target is currently masking nothing.

labels.shortDescription: Dead-config message: active target points to an inactive rule. {0}=target.
labels.value: Masking target "{0}" points to an inactive rule, so it is currently masking nothing. Reactivate the rule or deactivate this target.

labels.shortDescription: Dead-config message: active target points to a missing rule. {0}=target.
labels.value: Masking target "{0}" points to a rule that no longer exists, so it is currently masking nothing. Point it at an active rule or deactivate this target.

labels.shortDescription: Masking advisor bundle README body. {0}=members list or NoMembers fallback.
labels.value:
# Data Masking configuration
Generated by the Data Masking Advisor. Unzip this package, then deploy it to your org with the Salesforce CLI (no Salesforce project required):
sf project deploy start --metadata-dir metadata -o <your-org-alias>
Or import it with any tool that accepts metadata (change set, Workbench, your CI/CD pipeline).
Members: {0}

labels.shortDescription: Masking advisor bundle README: members-list fallback when bundle is empty.
Card refresh icon-button alt text.
Per-field masking-rule picker accessible label.
Shown in the rule picker when no rule matches the typed filter.
Per-field masking-rule picker search placeholder.
Export dialog dismiss (Close) action button.
Export change summary (plural); {0} total, {1} object, {2}/{3}/{4} counts.
labels.value: This package applies {0} changes to {1} when deployed ({2} add · {3} disable · {4} re-enable). Nothing changes in your org until you deploy it.

labels.shortDescription: Export change summary (1 change); {0} object, {1}/{2}/{3} counts.
labels.value: This package applies 1 change to {0} when deployed ({1} add · {2} disable · {3} re-enable). Nothing changes in your org until you deploy it.

labels.shortDescription: Export dialog copy-command button label after a successful copy.
Export dialog copy-deploy-command button label.
Placeholder shown in the deploy command before an org alias is entered.
Export dialog deploy path A (Salesforce CLI) intro line.
labels.value: Salesforce CLI (fastest): set your org alias, copy the command, and run it from the unzipped folder.

labels.shortDescription: Export dialog Salesforce CLI deploy command; {0}=org alias.
Accessible name for the focusable deploy-command block in the export dialog.
Export dialog deploy path B (your own tools) note.
labels.value: Your own tools: import the package with a change set, Workbench, or your CI/CD pipeline.

labels.shortDescription: Export dialog Download action button.
Export package download file name.
Export dialog empty package-contents placeholder.
Export dialog package row tag marking an object-wide target.
Export dialog org-alias input field label.
Export dialog org-alias input placeholder.
Export dialog package-contents list heading; {0}=total change count.
Export dialog modal title.
Export dialog package row verb tag for a created target.
Export dialog package row verb tag for a disabled target.
Export dialog package row verb tag for a re-enabled target.
Review dialog Apply button.
Review dialog Cancel button.
Review dialog label for the advisor's current classification.
Review dialog footer note explaining the change is session-only.
labels.value: Session-only — this moves the field and is included in the export package.

labels.shortDescription: Review dialog heading above the generic type-based content hints.
labels.value: Fields of this kind may contain

labels.shortDescription: Review dialog content hints for Address fields (pipe-delimited list).
Review dialog content hints for Date and Datetime fields (pipe-delimited list).
labels.value: Dates of birth or personal events

labels.shortDescription: Review dialog content hints fallback for unmapped field types.
Review dialog content hints for Picklist fields (pipe-delimited).
Review dialog content hints for Text fields (pipe-delimited).
Review dialog content hints for URL fields (pipe-delimited list).
Review dialog label for the classification radio group.
Review dialog suffix after the signal-band word, e.g. "Weak signal".
Review dialog modal title.
Review dialog label for the reason the field was flagged.
Display text for the NotSensitive classification.
Display text for the PossiblySensitive classification.
Display text for the Sensitive classification.
Display text for the Moderate match-strength band.
Display text for the Strong match-strength band.
Display text for the Weak match-strength band.
Scope-honesty banner body paragraph.
labels.value: Masking redacts sensitive values in two ways: as the framework's own data flows out (debug logs, outbound API callouts, async jobs, and platform events), and — for any object you configure with a masking target — on the record itself, just before it is inserted or updated. It is not encryption-at-rest: it does not change values that are already stored, and it does not restrict who can view a field beyond your field-level security.

labels.shortDescription: Inline example note shown when the rule masks keys inside a JSON payload.
labels.value: This rule masks keys inside a JSON payload. Paste JSON to see it redacted.

labels.shortDescription: Inline example note shown when the chosen rule left the sample value unchanged.
labels.value: This rule didn't change that value. Try a value that looks like real data.

labels.shortDescription: Inline example note shown when a sample-value preview could not be produced.
labels.value: Couldn't preview this value. Try again, or check the rule's pattern.

labels.shortDescription: Button that masks the admin's own sample value against the row's rule.
Accessible name for the sample-value input on a field row. {0} = field label.
Placeholder for the sample-value input on a field row.
Summary strip — count of fields ready to mask. {0} = count.
Summary strip — count of fields needing review. {0} = count.
Summary strip — count of other fields. {0} = count.
Ready-to-mask section heading. {0} = count.
Export package button label in the Ready-to-mask card header.
Needs-review section heading. {0} = count.
Needs-review section guidance copy.
labels.value: Ambiguous classifications. Tick a field to include it in the package, or open Review to reclassify it.

labels.shortDescription: Accessible name for the clickable sensitivity badge that opens Review.
Marker on a field the admin reclassified as not sensitive.
Inline action that undoes an in-place reclassification on a field.
Accessible name for a per-row Revert action (names the field).
Summary-strip count of in-place reclassifications made this session.
Toast shown when a field is reclassified as not sensitive.
Toast shown when an in-place reclassification is reverted.
Marker on a field the admin reclassified as sensitive to add it.
Toast when a reclassified field gains a rule and joins the package.
Toast when a reclassified field needs a rule before it can be added.
labels.value: {0} flagged for review. Add a rule to include it.

labels.shortDescription: Action that discards the saved reclassification + selection draft.
Toast confirming the saved draft was cleared.
Warning shown when the draft cannot be saved to the browser.
labels.value: Your changes can't be saved in this browser, so they'll be lost if you reload. Export now to keep them.

labels.shortDescription: Tag on an Other-table field flagged on a single weak heuristic signal.
Other table reason text when no sensitivity signal was detected.
Other table reason text when the field type suggests sensitivity.
labels.value: This field's type often holds sensitive data, but no rule matched its name.

labels.shortDescription: Toggle on the Other section that filters to likely-missed sensitive fields.
Other table cap note. {0} = shown, {1} = total.
labels.value: Showing the first {0} of {1} fields. Turn on Show likely misses only to narrow the list.

labels.shortDescription: Review landing heading for the org-wide masking posture summary.
Review posture note clarifying counts are as-configured, not a field scan.
labels.value: These counts reflect the masking targets configured in this org, not a scan of every field.

labels.shortDescription: Posture count label: distinct objects with at least one active target.
Posture count label: active targets that mask nothing (dead).
Review posture finding shown when the org has no active masking targets.
labels.value: Almost nothing is masked in this org yet. Pick an object to review its fields and start configuring masking.

labels.shortDescription: Heading above the list of masked objects on the Review landing.
Inventory row counts: object-wide and field-level totals.
Inventory row dead-configuration count suffix. {0} is the count.
Hint under the masked-objects list inviting the admin to open an object.
labels.value: Click an object to review its fields.

labels.shortDescription: Masking cell line label for object-wide rules that apply to this field.
Masking cell line label for this field's own rules and candidates.
Field table column header for the masking-rules cell.
Chip hint shown when unticking an active rule will disable it on deploy.
Chip hint shown when ticking an inactive rule will re-enable it on deploy.
Chip hint shown when a ticked candidate rule will be created on deploy.
Accessible label for a rule chip checkbox (masked after deploy).
Suffix appended to an inactive rule chip label.
Button that adds a field-level masking rule candidate.
Button that adds an object-wide masking rule candidate.
Object-wide banner heading when one active rule masks the object.
labels.value: {0} is masked object-wide by 1 active rule

labels.shortDescription: Object-wide banner heading when several active rules mask the object.
labels.value: {0} is masked object-wide by {1} active rules

labels.shortDescription: Object-wide banner heading when the object has no object-wide masking.
Toggle that expands a collapsed coverage banner.
Toggle that collapses an expanded coverage banner.
Explanatory note under the object-wide banner when active rules exist.
labels.value: These rules run on every field, redacting only the patterns they match. Untick one to disable it across the object. Some apply only to certain field types, so each field below lists the object-wide rules that apply to it.

labels.shortDescription: Explanatory note under the object-wide banner when no rules exist.
labels.value: An object-wide rule runs on every field of this object, redacting only the pattern it matches (for example credit-card numbers in any log field).

labels.shortDescription: Per-object configuration-health banner heading prefix.
Health summary fragment: one inactive object-wide rule.
Health summary fragment: several inactive object-wide rules.
Health summary fragment: one field target masking nothing.
Health summary fragment: several field targets masking nothing.
Health detail line for one inactive object-wide rule.
labels.value: 1 object-wide rule is configured across every field but masking nothing. Re-enable it in the object-wide banner below, or remove it in Setup.

labels.shortDescription: Health detail line for several inactive object-wide rules.
labels.value: {0} object-wide rules are configured across every field but masking nothing. Re-enable them in the object-wide banner below, or remove them in Setup.

labels.shortDescription: Health detail line for one field target that is masking nothing.
labels.value: Field-level rule {0} on this object is masking nothing.

labels.shortDescription: Hint shown when the export package has no queued changes.
labels.value: No changes queued. Tick a candidate to add masking, or untick an existing rule to disable it.

labels.shortDescription: Rule-detail status pill for a rule that is active.
Rule-detail status pill for a rule that is inactive.
Rule-detail status pill for a candidate rule not yet applied.
Rule-detail section heading describing what the rule redacts.
Rule-detail fallback when a rule records no description.
labels.value: No description is recorded for this rule.

labels.shortDescription: Rule-detail section heading for the sample-value test.
Rule-detail test prompt shown before a sample value is masked.
labels.value: Type a value, then select Try a value.

labels.shortDescription: Rule-detail section heading listing the field types the rule fits.
Rule-detail section heading showing where the rule is applied.
Rule-detail where-applied text for a field-level rule (object, field).
Rule-detail where-applied text for an object-wide rule (object).
Rule-detail where-applied text for a field-level candidate (object, field).
Rule-detail where-applied text for an object-wide candidate (object).
Rule-detail suffix flagging an inactive rule as masking nothing.
Rule-detail checkbox label for the masked-after-deploy desired state.
Rule-detail note: an active rule left ticked stays masked.
Rule-detail note: an active rule unticked is disabled on deploy.
Rule-detail note: an inactive rule ticked is re-enabled on deploy.
Rule-detail note: an inactive rule left unticked stays inactive.
Rule-detail note: a ticked candidate is added to masking on deploy.
Rule-detail note: an unticked candidate is not added on deploy.
Rule-detail footer note explaining ticked candidates are created on deploy.
labels.value: Ticked rules are created when you export and deploy this package.

labels.shortDescription: Rule-detail note for a read-only object-wide rule shown on a field.
labels.value: This rule is applied object-wide. Tick or untick it in the object-wide coverage banner, or open Setup to edit its pattern.

labels.shortDescription: Rule-detail footer link that opens the rule in Salesforce Setup.
Rule-detail footer button that closes the popup.
Friendly field-type name for a Text field.
Friendly field-type name for a Long Text Area field.
Friendly field-type name for a URL field.
Friendly field-type name for an Email field.
Friendly field-type name for a Phone field.
Friendly field-type name for an Encrypted Text field.
Friendly applies-to value for a rule that fits any text field.
Add-rule menu title for a field (takes the field label as {0}).
Add-rule menu title for an object (takes the object name as {0}).
Add-rule menu intro for a field.
labels.value: Rules that match this field's type. Adding one ticks it into the export package — nothing changes in your org until you deploy it.

labels.shortDescription: Add-rule menu intro for an object (takes the object name as {0}).
labels.value: An object-wide rule runs on every field of {0}, redacting only the patterns it matches. Adding one ticks it into the export package.

labels.shortDescription: Add-rule menu empty state when no rules match the field type.
labels.value: No other template rules match this field's type.

labels.shortDescription: Add-rule menu empty state when every rule is already object-wide.
labels.value: Every template rule is already applied object-wide here.

labels.shortDescription: Add-rule menu per-row button that adds the rule as a candidate.
Add-rule menu footer link that opens the New Masking Rule Setup page.
Add-rule menu footer button that closes the menu.
Add-rule menu line naming the field types a rule applies to.
Add-object-wide-rule warning when a rule matches no field on the object yet.
labels.value: Won't match any field on this object yet

labels.shortDescription: Glanceable chip marker for a rule another rule replaces.
Superseded masking rule annotation.
labels.value: Superseded by {0}. Existing bindings still actively mask their objects — deactivating this rule stops masking wherever it is still bound. Rebind those objects to {0} first.

labels.shortDescription: Publish error when the payload template for an event cannot be prepared.
labels.value: Unable to prepare a payload template for {0}. Enter the payload manually.

labels.shortDescription: Publish error shown when an event cannot be published.
labels.value: Unable to publish {0}. Check the payload values and format, then try again.

labels.shortDescription: Which event types can be published manually from the Publish screen.
labels.value: Only Generic and Custom Platform events can be published from here. Standard Platform, Change Data Capture, PushTopic, and Monitoring events are published by Salesforce or fire automatically when records change — to watch them, subscribe to their channel.

labels.shortDescription: Publish error toast body used when no detailed message is available.
Publish error toast title.
Publish success toast body.
Publish success toast title.
Publish error when the named generic streaming channel does not exist.
labels.value: No streaming channel named {0} exists in this org.

labels.shortDescription: Publish error when the named platform event does not exist.
labels.value: No platform event named {0} exists in this org.

labels.shortDescription: Publish error when the event type cannot be published.
labels.value: Events of type {0} cannot be published from the Event Monitor.

labels.shortDescription: Accessible label for the event timeline chart.
Timeline dot tooltip call-to-action.
Event usage metrics card title.
Usage metrics badge when no rows exist.
Usage metrics badge, all data points visible. {0} = count.
Usage metrics badge, subset visible. {0} = visible, {1} = total.
Date range preset: custom range.
Custom range lower-bound input label.
Date range preset picker label.
Date range preset: last 24 hours (Hourly default).
Date range preset: last 30 days (Daily default).
Date range preset: last 7 days.
Date range preset: last hour (FifteenMinutes default).
Custom range upper-bound input label.
Usage metrics empty-state guidance.
labels.value: Try a wider date range or a coarser granularity.

labels.shortDescription: Usage metrics empty-state heading.
labels.value: No usage data for the selected range

labels.shortDescription: Notice shown when Enhanced Usage Metrics is off. Link label appended.
labels.value: Hourly and 15-minute granularity require the Enhanced Usage Metrics feature. This org reports daily totals only.

labels.shortDescription: Help-link text inside the Enhanced Usage Metrics notice.
Usage metrics load-failure message.
labels.value: Couldn't load usage metrics. The request to the server failed — your data hasn't changed.

labels.shortDescription: Usage metrics load-failure retry button.
Granularity option: daily buckets.
Granularity option: fifteen-minute buckets.
Granularity option: hourly buckets.
Granularity segmented-control label.
Minimum Daily query range (one bucket).
Minimum FifteenMinutes query range (one bucket).
Minimum Hourly query range (one bucket).
Empty-state reset action.
Table view first column header.
Custom range validation: From must precede To.
labels.value: The "From" time must be earlier than the "To" time.

labels.shortDescription: Usage metrics range-too-narrow error. {0} = minimum range.
labels.value: The selected date range is too narrow for the chosen granularity — minimum is {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Usage metrics range-too-wide error. {0} = maximum window.
labels.value: The selected date range is too wide for the chosen granularity — maximum is {0}.

labels.shortDescription: Usage metrics error when the start date is after the end date.
labels.value: The start date must be on or before the end date.

labels.shortDescription: Usage metrics error when an unsupported time segment is requested.
labels.value: The selected time segment is not supported.

labels.shortDescription: View switch option: chart.
View switch group label.
View switch option: table.
Max query window for daily granularity (range-too-wide message).
Max query window for fifteen-minute granularity (range-too-wide msg).
Max query window for hourly granularity (range-too-wide message).
Org limits card and sidebar-nav title.
Notice above the org-limits grid.
labels.value: Publishing or subscribing in the Streaming Monitor uses some of these limits.

labels.shortDescription: Shown when the org limits fail to load.
labels.value: Org limits could not be loaded.

labels.shortDescription: Empty state when no limit matches the search.
Placeholder for the org-limits search box.
Label for the org-limits sort combobox.
Default sort option: highest usage first.
Alphabetical sort option.
Usage line: {0} used of {1} maximum.
Mixed-banner headline when a check is failing
Health Check headline when every check passed
Health Check suffix when multiple checks passed. {0} = number of checks.
Health Check suffix when a single check passed
Health Check card title
Class Type Resolver health check display name
Resolver references a class that cannot be found
labels.value: A class type resolver is configured but the class it references cannot be found. Check that the class is deployed and accessible. Until it is, Kern cannot locate your public trigger handlers, validation rules, scheduled jobs, or web service classes — you would need to declare them as global to work around it.

labels.shortDescription: Detail message when a Class Type Resolver is configured and working
labels.value: A class type resolver is configured and working.

labels.shortDescription: Resolver class is not compatible
labels.value: A class type resolver is configured but the class it references is not compatible. The resolver class must extend the Kern base resolver. Until it does, Kern cannot use it to locate your public classes in your own namespace.

labels.shortDescription: Detail message when no Class Type Resolver is configured
labels.value: No class type resolver is configured. Kern lives in its own managed-package namespace and can only find subscriber Apex classes declared as global. If you'd rather keep your trigger handlers, validation rules, scheduled jobs, and web service classes as public (recommended), add a class type resolver so Kern can locate them in your own namespace.

labels.shortDescription: Custom-object masking coverage health check display name
Custom objects show no unmasked sensitive fields; {0} = checked count
labels.value: Your custom objects show no likely-sensitive fields without masking ({0} checked).

labels.shortDescription: Suffix counting custom objects one run did not check; {0} = count
labels.value: {0} more custom objects were not checked in this pass — open the Data Masking Advisor to scan everything.

labels.shortDescription: Warn for unmasked sensitive custom objects; {0} count, {1} names
labels.value: {0} of your custom objects hold likely-sensitive fields with no masking configured: {1}.

labels.shortDescription: Apply Recommended Retention button label (mirrors ModalTitle value)
Data retention health check name
Data retention configured detail
labels.value: Scheduled purge jobs are configured for framework data objects.

labels.shortDescription: Customize sub-row Configured pill label
Create N jobs button label (takes N as {0})
Back to one-click apply link label
Customize each job link label
Customize-mode headline; {0} = number of jobs being customised
Customize-mode helper copy rendered under the headline
labels.value: Each Set up button opens the scheduled job editor with recommended defaults prefilled. Tweak anything before saving.

labels.shortDescription: First-run deletion warning
labels.value: Records older than the retention period will be deleted on the first scheduled run.

labels.shortDescription: Data retention missing detail with object list
labels.value: No data retention configured for {0}. Without scheduled purge jobs, these records accumulate indefinitely and consume storage.

labels.shortDescription: Apply Recommended Retention modal subtitle ({0} = number of jobs)
labels.value: Kern will create {0} active scheduled jobs that delete records older than the recommended retention period. Pick a schedule per row, or customize each job instead for a fully custom cron.

labels.shortDescription: Apply Recommended Retention modal title
Schedule picklist for apply-retention modal (pipe-delimited label=cron)
labels.value: Nightly 2:00 AM=0 0 2 * * ?|Nightly 1:00 AM=0 0 1 * * ?|Weekly Sunday 2:00 AM=0 0 2 ? * SUN|Monthly 1st at 2:00 AM=0 0 2 1 * ?

labels.shortDescription: Default SchedulerName__c for the API Call purge job
Default SchedulerName__c for the API Issue purge job
Default SchedulerName__c for the Async Chain Execution purge job
Default SchedulerName__c for the Log Entry purge job
Customize sub-row Set up button label
Success toast message (takes N as {0})
Section heading for Fail-severity action items
Display name for the Data Masking posture health check
Warn detail when masking targets mask nothing. {0} = dead target count.
labels.value: {0} masking target(s) are masking nothing — open the advisor to fix or remove them.

labels.shortDescription: Pass detail. {0} = count of objects with active masking.
Warn detail when no object carries active masking yet
labels.value: Almost nothing is masked in this org yet — open the advisor to review your objects.

labels.shortDescription: Detail message when Organisation Cache is allocated. {0} = partition name.
labels.value: Organisation Cache is allocated to the {0} partition.

labels.shortDescription: Organisation Cache health check display name
Detail message when Organisation Cache is not allocated. {0} = partition name.
labels.value: Allocate Organisation Cache to the {0} partition (Setup > Platform Cache > Edit). Kern uses this cache to remember state between requests — without it, automatic retries on failing integrations, protection from repeated errors, and shared configuration lookups stop working.

labels.shortDescription: Health Check label prefix for the passing chip row
Health Check refresh button assistive text
Action button label that opens the Data Masking Advisor
Mixed-banner headline when only warnings are present
Data Retention action button label linking to Scheduled Jobs
Detail message when Session Cache is allocated. {0} = partition name.
labels.value: Session Cache is allocated to the {0} partition.

labels.shortDescription: Session Cache health check display name
Detail message when Session Cache is not allocated. {0} = partition name.
labels.value: Allocate Session Cache to the {0} partition (Setup > Platform Cache > Edit). Kern uses this to store per-user data (like encryption keys) close to each user's session. Without it, that data falls back to the shared Org Cache — using more of your org-cache allocation and slowing down user-specific operations.

labels.shortDescription: Health Check action-item Setup button label
Trusted Site health check display name
Trusted URL configured detail
Trusted URL missing detail
labels.value: Add a Trusted URL for {0} (Setup > Trusted URLs > New Trusted URL). Some Kern features call back into your own org — for example, pushing to streaming channels and other self-service API operations. Salesforce blocks these calls unless your org domain is explicitly trusted.

labels.shortDescription: Section heading for Warn-severity action items
Kern Home administration tools section heading
API Test Harness tool card description
labels.value: Test inbound and outbound API calls with Safe Mode and Mocking control.

labels.shortDescription: API Test Harness tool card title
Chain Monitor tool card description
labels.value: Monitor async chain executions, view step timelines, and diagnose failures in real time.

labels.shortDescription: Chain Monitor tool card title
Data Masking Advisor tool card description
labels.value: Surface sensitive fields on a chosen object, preview masking rule output, and build a deployable masking configuration bundle.

labels.shortDescription: Data Masking Advisor tool card title
Log Console tool card description
labels.value: Browse and group log entries by distinct problem, see a per-level summary, and open a tabbed detail drawer for any entry.

labels.shortDescription: Log Console tool card title
Streaming Event Monitor tool card description
labels.value: Monitor platform events, change data capture, and custom streaming channels in real time.

labels.shortDescription: Streaming Event Monitor tool card title
Shared launch/open button label for tool cards
Detail action: export the entry's transaction
Detail action: hide this signature for the session
Detail action: open the entry in Chain Monitor
Detail action: open the standard log entry record
Detail action: suppress capture of this class
Detail action label when suppress is unavailable for ERROR
Chain tab heading above the ordered list of correlated entries
Explains what the correlation chain shows
labels.value: The operation end to end: one user action followed across triggers, queueables, callouts and the async chain. The correlation id is the spine. Select any node or row to jump to that entry.

labels.shortDescription: Section heading for the correlation chain strip
Relative offset of a chain node, in hours from the first node
Relative offset of a chain node, in minutes from the first node
Relative offset of a chain node, in seconds from the first node
Shown in the Chain tab when no other entry shares the correlation
labels.value: Only this entry shares this correlation.

labels.shortDescription: Offset label for the first node in the correlation chain
Marks the opened entry's own node in the correlation chain
Overview-tab link that opens the full correlation chain view
Close the detail drawer
Confirmation shown after copying to the clipboard
Copy the code block to the clipboard
Error toast shown when copying to the clipboard fails
labels.value: Couldn't copy to the clipboard. Try again or copy the text manually.

labels.shortDescription: Detail Overview section heading for the metadata grid
Label preceding the first-seen timestamp
Label preceding the last-seen timestamp
Detail Overview heading for the governor-limit bars
Note that only consumed governor limits are shown
Metadata grid label for the execution context
Metadata grid label for the correlation id
Metadata grid label for the log number
Metadata grid label for the parent transaction id
Metadata grid label for the source class and method
Metadata grid label for the transaction id
Metadata grid label for the originating user
Assistive label for the detail overflow actions menu
Empty state when no governor limits were consumed
Empty state when the entry has no stack trace
labels.value: No stack trace for this entry.

labels.shortDescription: Detail tab label for the correlation chain
Detail tab label for the context JSON
Detail tab label for the overview
Detail tab label for the stack trace
Default option for the execution-context filter
Banner shown when the result set hit the candidate cap
labels.value: Showing the first {0}. Narrow the filters to see the rest.

labels.shortDescription: Reset all filters
Clear all session-hidden signatures
List column header for the execution context
List column header for the correlation id
List column header for the occurrence count
List column header for the last-seen time (problems view)
List column header for the log level
List column header for the log number
List column header for the message (entries view)
List column header for the problem (problems view)
List column header for the source class and method
List column header for the entry time (entries view)
Assistive label for the execution-context filter
Summary footnote suffix for the distinct count shown
Per-level summary footnote suffix for the distinct count
Empty state when no logs match the filters
Banner counting session-hidden signatures
Note explaining that session-hide is temporary
Toggle to re-hide session-hidden signatures
Assistive text for the loading spinner
Per-level summary card label suffix
Top-source summary footnote suffix
Manual refresh button
Placeholder for the message/source/correlation search
Toggle to reveal session-hidden signatures
Console brand-bar title
Summary card label for the top source class
Ribbon card heading for the ranked top source classes
All-entries ribbon total card label for forensic rows matching the filters
All-entries ribbon total card footnote — the unit of the total count
Detail header label preceding a single entry's logged timestamp (entries view)
Detail Overview placeholder shown when the entry has no message body
All-entries ribbon per-level card footnote and top-source metric unit
Summary card label for total occurrences
Badge on the ribbon total when folded counts are best-effort
Tooltip explaining the approximate-totals badge
labels.value: This window holds more activity than the console counts at once. Totals include only the most recent activity groups and may be higher.

labels.shortDescription: View toggle for the flat entries list
View toggle for the grouped-problem summary list
Execution-context display name for Anonymous Apex
Execution-context display name for Aura / LWC controller calls
Execution-context display name for Batch Apex
Execution-context display name for future methods
Execution-context display name for Queueable jobs
Execution-context display name for a Queueable finalizer
Execution-context display name for Apex REST
Execution-context display name for Scheduled Apex
Execution-context display name for a synchronous transaction
Assistive label for the log-level filter button group
Date-range picklist label
Date-range preset: last 15 minutes
Date-range preset: last hour
Date-range preset: last 4 hours
Date-range preset: last 24 hours
Date-range preset: last 7 days
Date-range preset: last 30 days
Date-range calendar preset: today so far
Date-range calendar preset: the whole of yesterday
Date-range calendar preset: this week so far
Date-range calendar preset: the whole of last week
Date-range calendar preset: this month so far
Date-range preset that reveals the custom from/to date and time inputs
Custom date-range lower-bound date input label
Custom date-range lower-bound time input label
Custom date-range upper-bound date input label
Custom date-range upper-bound time input label
Link from a scan-sourced object screen back to the scan results.
Cancel button for a running custom-object coverage scan.
Footnote counting other custom objects with no likely-sensitive fields.
labels.value: {0} other custom objects show no likely-sensitive fields.

labels.shortDescription: All-clear message when no custom object needs attention after a scan.
labels.value: No custom object shows likely-sensitive fields without masking.

labels.shortDescription: Footnote beneath the flagged scan rows guiding the next action.
labels.value: Click an object to review its fields and export a masking package for it.

labels.shortDescription: Heading of the custom-object coverage scan section on the advisor landing.
Idle-state explanation of what the coverage scan does.
labels.value: The scan runs the advisor's field analysis on each object, a few at a time, and lists the ones with likely-sensitive fields and no masking. You can cancel at any point and keep what was found. Nothing changes in your org.

labels.shortDescription: Toggle to include managed-package objects. {0} is the managed-object count.
labels.value: Include managed-package objects ({0} from installed packages)

labels.shortDescription: Running-state note of how many objects are assessed at once.
Idle-state introduction to why custom objects need a masking review.
labels.value: Your own custom objects can hold the same kind of sensitive data the framework masks by default — emails, phone numbers, free-text bodies — but masking only covers them once you configure it.

labels.shortDescription: Suffix marking a flagged field as a Long Text Area.
Idle-state note when the org has no own custom objects to scan.
labels.value: You have no custom objects of your own to scan.

labels.shortDescription: Pill on a flagged scan row indicating the object has no masking configured.
Note after a cancelled scan. {0} assessed, {1} the population total.
labels.value: Scan cancelled after {0} of {1} objects — results below are partial.

labels.shortDescription: Idle-state note of how many own custom objects exist. {0} is the count.
labels.value: You have {0} custom objects of your own.

labels.shortDescription: Clause about excluded managed-package objects. {0} is the count.
labels.value: {0} more come from managed packages — they belong to the package vendor and are excluded unless you opt in below.

labels.shortDescription: Banner when an object is opened from a scan result. {0} flagged-field count.
labels.value: Flagged by the custom-object scan — {0} likely-sensitive fields below have no masking.

labels.shortDescription: Button to re-run the custom-object coverage scan.
Idle-state button starting the scan. {0} is the population total.
Running-state progress line. {0} is the object, {1} the position, {2} the total.
Completed-scan summary. {0} assessed, {1} need attention, {2} flagged fields.
labels.value: Scanned {0} custom objects · {1} need attention · {2} likely-sensitive fields with no masking

labels.shortDescription: Landing button that opens the grouped add-object search dialog.
Field-screen link back to the posture landing (clears the selected object).
Add-object dialog title.
Add-object dialog intro line.
labels.value: Pick the object whose fields you want to review and mask. Opening an object changes nothing in your org.

labels.shortDescription: Placeholder for the add-object dialog search box.
Add-object group heading for scan-flagged objects.
Add-object group heading for the subscriber's own custom objects.
Add-object group heading for standard objects.
Add-object group heading for managed-package objects. {0} is the count.
Pill on a scan-flagged add-object row.
Pill on an add-object row whose object already has active masking.
Sampled-standard-list hint. {0} shown of {1} total.
labels.value: Showing {0} of {1} standard objects — type to narrow.

labels.shortDescription: Sampled-managed-list hint. {0} shown of {1} total.
labels.value: Showing {0} of {1} managed-package objects — type to narrow.

labels.shortDescription: Expander that reveals the collapsed managed-package group. {0} is the count.
Empty-state message when no object matches the search term {0}.
Close button on the add-object dialog.
Title of the regulated-field inventory export dialog.
Intro text for the inventory export dialog.
labels.value: A spreadsheet-ready inventory of fields that hold (or likely hold) regulated data — what they are, how they are classified, whether masking covers them today, and where history tracking is worth turning on. Read-only: nothing changes in your org.

labels.shortDescription: Scope section heading in the inventory export dialog.
Custom-objects scope option in the inventory export dialog.
Masked-objects scope option in the inventory export dialog.
All-objects scope option in the inventory export dialog.
Singular object-count suffix on an export scope option.
Plural object-count suffix on an export scope option.
Format section heading in the inventory export dialog.
CSV format option in the inventory export dialog.
JSON format option in the inventory export dialog.
Note explaining the CSV-vs-JSON value contract in the inventory export dialog.
labels.value: CSV uses readable labels for a spreadsheet; JSON keeps the raw API values (field types, rule names, true/false) for tooling.

labels.shortDescription: Masked-objects scope note in the inventory export dialog.
labels.value: These objects are masked object-wide, so only fields independently flagged as regulated appear here, not a row per field.

labels.shortDescription: Sensitive-only filter checkbox label in the inventory export dialog.
Note shown when the sensitive-only filter removes every row.
labels.value: No fields are classified Sensitive in this scope. Clear the filter to include possibly-sensitive fields.

labels.shortDescription: Singular columns/row-count heading in the export preview.
Plural columns/row-count heading in the export preview.
Singular remaining-rows note under the export preview.
Plural remaining-rows note under the export preview.
Object column header in the inventory export.
Field column header in the inventory export.
Field Type column header in the inventory export.
Long Text Area column header in the inventory export.
Security Classification column header in the export.
Compliance Group column header in the inventory export.
Sensitivity column header in the inventory export.
Recommended Rule column header in the inventory export.
Masked Today column header in the inventory export.
History Tracking Recommended column header in the export.
Yes cell value in the inventory export.
No cell value in the inventory export.
Empty-value placeholder in the inventory export preview.
Progress note while the export scope is analyzed.
Confirm body before analyzing the all-objects export scope.
labels.value: This will analyze all {0} objects and may take a minute.

labels.shortDescription: Confirm button to build the all-objects export preview.
Empty state when an export scope has no regulated fields.
labels.value: No regulated fields in this scope.

labels.shortDescription: Singular skipped-object note in the inventory export.
labels.value: {0} object could not be analyzed and was skipped.

labels.shortDescription: Plural skipped-object note in the inventory export.
labels.value: {0} objects could not be analyzed and were skipped.

labels.shortDescription: Download button label in the inventory export dialog.
Close button on the inventory export dialog.
File name for the CSV inventory export.
File name for the JSON inventory export.
Posture-section button that opens the inventory export dialog.
Detail tab label for the correlated execution timeline
Timeline header title when the entry is correlated
Timeline header title when the entry has no correlation
Timeline note shown when the entry has no correlation id
labels.value: No correlation id, so this entry stands alone.

labels.shortDescription: Timeline note shown when correlated but not a registered async chain
labels.value: Correlated by id {0}. These entries share one operation, but it is not a registered async chain, so there is no Chain Monitor view.

labels.shortDescription: Label for the timeline overview minimap section
Singular transaction noun for the timeline overview stat
Plural transactions noun for the timeline overview stat
Singular entry noun for the timeline counts
Plural entries noun for the timeline counts
Timeline overview marker naming the transaction that errored
Minimap label for a collapsed run of async transactions
Timeline breadcrumb naming the parent transaction that spawned this hop
Timeline marker shown when a hop starts well after its parent ended
labels.value: {0} gap before this hop; it was queued, or a step may be missing.

labels.shortDescription: Timeline cap row when more transactions exist than are shown
labels.value: Showing the first {0} of {1} transactions. Open in Chain Monitor for the full chain.

labels.shortDescription: Timeline cap row when the server truncated a very long trace
labels.value: This trace is very long, so only its earliest entries are shown here. Open in Chain Monitor for the full chain.

labels.shortDescription: Marker on the timeline row that is the opened entry
Focus card note when the hop has no stack trace or context
labels.value: No stack trace or context captured for this entry.

labels.shortDescription: Focus card state while the hop detail loads
Focus card state when the hop detail could not be loaded
labels.value: Couldn't load this entry's detail. Open the record for the full detail.

labels.shortDescription: Timeline legend text for the debug severity dot
Timeline legend text for the info severity dot
Timeline legend text for the warning severity dot
Timeline legend text for the error severity dot
Timeline legend text for the opened-entry dot
Duration unit: hours component of a timeline span
Duration unit: minutes component of a timeline span
Duration unit: seconds component of a timeline span
Detail card error message when the scheduled job record fails to load
labels.value: The scheduled job could not be loaded.

labels.shortDescription: Detail card loading spinner assistive text
Title shown when creating a scheduled job
Title shown when editing a scheduled job
Schedule section heading
Schedule heading with the user timezone in parentheses
Error toast when the scheduled job record fails to load
Error toast when parameter definitions fail to load
Success toast after creating a scheduled job
Success toast after saving a scheduled job
Error toast when saving a scheduled job fails
Field label for the scheduler name
Field label for the Apex class name
Placeholder for the class-name lookup
Active state label (toggle and status display)
Inactive state label (toggle and status display)
Parameters section heading
Boolean parameter toggle on-label
Boolean parameter toggle off-label
Field label for the job description
Cancel button label
Save button label
Detail card title
Status field label
Empty-state text shown when no cron schedule is configured
Parameters table parameter-name column header
Parameters table value column header
Success toast title after saving from the editor modal
Preset frequency option: every N minutes
Preset frequency option: hourly
Preset frequency option: daily
Preset frequency option: weekly
Preset frequency option: monthly
Minute-interval option: every 5 minutes
Minute-interval option: every 10 minutes
Minute-interval option: every 15 minutes
Minute-interval option: every 30 minutes
Day name: Sunday
Day name: Monday
Day name: Tuesday
Day name: Wednesday
Day name: Thursday
Day name: Friday
Day name: Saturday
Editor mode option: Preset
Editor mode option: Advanced
Editor mode option: Custom
Mode radio-group label
Frequency combobox label
Minute-interval combobox label
Hourly at-minute combobox label
Preset hour combobox label
Preset minute combobox label
Weekly days checkbox-group label
Day-of-month field label
Advanced seconds combobox label
Advanced minutes input label
Advanced hours input label
Advanced month input label
Advanced day-of-week field-level help
Advanced day-of-week input label
Advanced year field-level help
Advanced year input label
Custom-mode cron expression input label
Preview section label
Warning toast when a cron expression cannot map to Preset mode
labels.value: This expression cannot be represented in Preset mode. Use Advanced or Custom mode instead.

labels.shortDescription: Ordinal: first occurrence in month
Ordinal: second occurrence in month
Ordinal: third occurrence in month
Ordinal: fourth occurrence in month
Ordinal: fifth occurrence in month
Day-list description joining exactly two days
Day-list description conjunction joining the final day ({0})
Description: every N minutes
Description: hourly at a given minute
Description: daily at a given time
Description: daily within a given month at a time
Description: nth weekday of every month at a time
Description: last named weekday of every month
Description: every weekday at a time
Description: a day range at a time
Description: an explicit day list at a time
Description: last day of every month
labels.value: Last day of every month at {0}

labels.shortDescription: Description: last weekday of every month
labels.value: Last weekday of every month at {0}

labels.shortDescription: Description suffix qualifying a specific month
Description: nearest weekday to a day of month
labels.value: Nearest weekday to day {0} of every month{1} at {2}

labels.shortDescription: Description: monthly on a specific day
Validation help for the seconds field
Validation help for the minutes field
Validation help for the hours field
Validation help for the day-of-month field
labels.value: Enter 1-31, *, ?, L, W, LW, */N, N/N, or comma/range lists

labels.shortDescription: Validation help for the month field
Validation help for the day-of-week field
Validation help for the year field
labels.value: Enter a year (1970-2099) or leave empty

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: year is in the past
labels.value: Year {0} is in the past — the job will never fire

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: value out of range
Validation error: # expression day out of range
Validation error: # expression occurrence out of range
Validation error: step below minimum
labels.value: Step value must be at least 1

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: step above maximum
Validation error: step base out of range
labels.value: Base value {0} is out of range ({1}-{2})

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: reversed range
labels.value: Range {0}-{1} is invalid — start must not exceed end

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: day impossible for the month
labels.value: Day {0} does not exist in {1} (max {2} days)

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: both day fields are ?
labels.value: Exactly one of Day of Month or Day of Week must be ? — both cannot be ?

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: neither day field is ?
labels.value: Exactly one of Day of Month or Day of Week must be ? — set one field to ?

labels.shortDescription: Validation error: wrong number of fields
labels.value: Invalid cron expression — must have 6 or 7 space-separated fields

labels.shortDescription: Card title for the full-page Chain Monitor
Status filter option: chain execution is running
Status filter option: chain execution completed
Status filter option: chain execution failed
Status filter option: chain execution was aborted
Status filter option: chain execution stalled
Chain name label (list column and search filter)
Status label (list column and status filter)
List column header for step progress
Start-time label (list column and detail field)
Duration label (list column and detail field)
Button to clear all status filters
Button to select all status filters
Pagination position, e.g. Page 1 of 3
Placeholder for the chain-name search input
Pagination button to go to the previous page
Pagination button to go to the next page
Progress badge beside the chain status, e.g. 3/5 steps
Success toast when the correlation ID is copied
Accordion section title for the chain steps
Accordion section title for chain timing
Timing field label for the completion time
Timing field label for the correlation ID
Accordion section title for the error details
Empty-state heading when no chain is selected
Empty-state guidance when no chain is selected
labels.value: Choose a chain execution from the list to view step details.

labels.shortDescription: Heading for the active subscriptions list
Header button to clear all received events
Header menu label to choose the event view mode
View-mode option: show events on a timeline
Streaming Monitor title (shared: sidebar nav and events card)
Subscribe-to-a-channel title (shared: sidebar nav and subscribe card)
Sidebar collapse/expand toggle title and assistive text
Sidebar section heading for streaming actions
Sidebar nav item: subscribe to multiple channels
Sidebar nav item: publish an event
Sidebar nav item: register an event source
Sidebar section heading for dashboards
Filter label: include events on a channel
Placeholder for the channel filter dropdown
Filter label: include events whose payload matches a keyword
Placeholder for the payload keyword filter
Filter checkbox: make the payload match case-sensitive
Filter toggle: include events after a time
Label for the after-time filter input
Filter toggle: include events before a time
Label for the before-time filter input
Button to clear all streaming event filters
Event type name for PushTopic events.
Event type name for generic events.
Event type name for standard platform events.
Event type name for custom platform events.
Event type name for Change Data Capture events.
Event type name for custom-channel platform events.
Event type name for custom-channel change events.
Event type name for monitoring events.
Event grid type label for a received PushTopic event ({0} = topic type).
Event grid type label for a received generic event.
Event grid type label for a Change event ({0}=entity, {1}=change).
Event grid type label for a received platform event.
Event grid type label for an unrecognised event.
Fallback streaming-error message when no error detail is available.
labels.value: See browser's dev console for details

labels.shortDescription: Subscribe error when a CDC channel may be inactive ({0} = channel).
labels.value: Failed to subscribe to {0}. Is the Change Data Capture event active?

labels.shortDescription: Subscribe error when denied by policy ({0} = channel, {1} = reason).
Toast title for a runtime streaming API error.
Toast title when no channels match the subscribe filter.
Toast body when no channels match the subscribe filter.
labels.value: There are no channels to subscribe to with the specified filter and current subscriptions

labels.shortDescription: Toast title after subscribing to multiple channels.
Toast body after subscribing to multiple channels.
labels.value: Successfully subscribed to the specified channels

labels.shortDescription: Toast title when a duplicate channel subscription is rejected.
Toast body when already subscribed to a channel ({0} = channel).
Toast title after subscribing to a single channel.
Toast title when a streaming event arrives on a channel.
Toast title after unsubscribing from all channels.
Toast body after unsubscribing from all channels.
Toast title after unsubscribing from a single channel.
Toast title when unsubscribing from a channel fails.
Field label for an event's local time (table column and detail).
Field label for an event's channel (table column and detail).
Field label for an event's type (table column and detail).
Field label for an event's replay id (table column and detail).
Field label for an event's payload (table column and detail).
Row-action tooltip to open the event detail modal.
Title of the event detail modal.
Notice shown when active filters may hide incoming events.
labels.value: Current filters may prevent incoming events from being displayed.

labels.shortDescription: Event count badge when all events are shown ({0} = count).
Event count badge when filtered ({0} = shown, {1} = total).
Empty-state message before any subscription exists.
Empty-state message while subscribed but no events yet.
Empty-state message when filters hide all events.
labels.value: No events displayed. Try changing your filters.

labels.shortDescription: Combobox label for choosing an event type.
Placeholder for the event-type combobox.
Combobox label for choosing an event name.
Input label for the streaming channel path.
Combobox label for choosing a replay option.
Input label for a custom replay ID.
Combobox label for the subscribe-all channel filter.
Textarea label for the event payload to publish.
Button that starts a channel subscription.
Button that publishes the entered event.
Card title of the register-event-source screen.
Card title of the subscribe-to-multiple-channels screen.
Card title of the publish-event screen.
Note on the subscribe-all screen about supported channels.
labels.value: Custom Change Data Capture channels cannot be automatically discovered. Supported channels: PushTopic, Generic, Platform events, Change Data Capture events and Monitoring events.

labels.shortDescription: Intro line above the PushTopic Apex registration sample.
labels.value: Customize and execute the following code in Apex to register a new PushTopic:

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text instructions for registering a generic event.
labels.value: Navigate to the  Streaming Channel tab to register a generic event.

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text instructions for registering a custom platform event.
labels.value: Navigate to the  Platform Event page in Setup to register a custom platform event.

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text note that standard platform events need no registration.
labels.value: Standard platform events do not need to be registered. See  documentation.

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text instructions for activating a change data capture event.
labels.value: Navigate to the  Change Data Capture page in Setup to activate a change data capture event.

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text instructions for declaring a custom platform-event channel.
labels.value: Custom channels cannot be declared via the user interface. Follow these  metadata deploy instructions.

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text instructions for declaring a custom change-event channel.
labels.value: Custom channels cannot be declared via the user interface. Follow these  metadata deploy instructions.

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text notice that Event Manager streaming must be enabled.
labels.value: Streaming must be enabled in  Event Manager in order to receive Monitoring Events.

labels.shortDescription: Rich-text notice about enabling Change Data Capture events in Setup.
labels.value: Enable Change Data Capture events in  Setup to receive them on the /data/ChangeEvents channel or the single-event channels. This doesn't apply to custom channels you define.

labels.shortDescription: Event-name placeholder when channels are available.
Event-name placeholder before an event type is chosen.
Event-name placeholder for custom CDC channels (manual input).
labels.value: The /data/ChangeEvents channel and custom channels require manual channel input

labels.shortDescription: Event-name placeholder for custom channels (manual input).
labels.value: Custom channels require manual channel input

labels.shortDescription: Event-name placeholder when a type has no channels ({0} = type label).
Validation message when the publish payload is not valid JSON.
Replay option that receives only new events.
Replay option that replays past events.
Replay option that starts from a custom replay ID.
Subscribe-all filter option for every event.
Subscribe-all filter option for custom events only.
Subscribe-all filter option for one event type ({0} = type label).
Payload help text for generic events.
Payload help text for JSON-payload events.
labels.value: JSON formatted payload with strings delimited by double quotes

labels.shortDescription: Direction option for outbound API calls.
Direction option for inbound API calls.
Mode badge when Safe Mode and Mocking are both off.
Mode badge when Safe Mode is off but Mocking is on.
Mode badge when Safe Mode and Mocking are both on.
Safe Mode toggle label, mode badge, and result badge.
Execute button label when Safe Mode is on.
Execute button label when Safe Mode is off.
Status badge fallback when the result has no status.
Validation error when a parameter has a value but no key.
labels.value: Parameter key cannot be blank when a value is provided.

labels.shortDescription: Validation error when the JSON body does not parse.
Toast when an execution completes successfully.
Toast fallback when an execution is aborted.
Toast fallback when an execution fails.
Toast after copying the result summary.
Toast after copying the request headers.
Toast after copying the response headers.
Toast after copying the request body.
Toast after copying the response body.
Toast after copying the error messages.
Toast when copying to the clipboard fails.
Copied-summary service line ({0} = service name).
Copied-summary status line ({0} = status, {1} = status code).
Copied-summary timing line ({0}-{3} = total/handler/callout/commit).
Copied-summary Safe Mode line ({0} = Enabled/Disabled).
Copied-summary mocked line ({0} = Yes/No).
Copied-summary API call line ({0} = record id).
Copied-summary word for an enabled setting.
Copied-summary word for a disabled setting.
Copied-summary affirmative word.
Copied-summary negative word.
Label for the direction radio group.
Label for the service combobox.
Required-field message for the service combobox.
Placeholder for the service combobox.
Label for the triggering record ID input.
Placeholder for the triggering record ID input.
Heading above the outbound parameter rows.
Label and placeholder for a parameter key input.
Parameter value input and header-table value column.
Header-table name column.
Button that adds a parameter row.
Label for the inbound JSON body textarea.
Placeholder for the inbound JSON body textarea.
Heading of the execution settings safety bar.
Safe Mode toggle message when on.
Safe Mode toggle message when off.
Label for the mocking toggle.
Mocking toggle message when on.
Mocking toggle message when off.
Button that resets the harness form.
Result badge shown for a mocked response.
Result metric tile for the HTTP status code.
Result metric tile for the total execution time.
Result metric tile for the handler duration.
Result metric tile for the callout duration.
Result metric tile for the commit duration.
Result metric tile for the ApiCall record id.
Tab label for the request details.
Heading of the request headers table.
Heading of the request body viewer.
Tab label for the response details.
Heading of the response headers table.
Heading of the response body viewer.
Tab label for the error details.
Heading of the error messages block.
Message when a result has no request or response data.
labels.value: No request or response data available.

labels.shortDescription: Empty-state heading before the first execution.
Empty-state body before the first execution.
labels.value: Configure the request on the left and click Execute.

labels.shortDescription: Toast when retrying a failed API call succeeds.
Toast fallback when retrying a failed API call fails again.
Toast fallback when the retry action throws unexpectedly.
Retention-proposal table column for the target object.
Retention-proposal table column for today's record count.
Retention-proposal table column for the retention period.
Retention-proposal schedule column and picklist label.
Retention period cell ({0} = number of days).
Button that closes the retention modal without applying.
Headline word for a single warning.
Headline word for multiple warnings.
Toast when refreshing the retention proposals fails.
Retention sub-row record count for exactly one record.
Retention sub-row record count ({0} = formatted count).
Retention sub-row period text ({0} = number of days).
Empty state for a lookup with no matching records.
Title of the button that clears the selected lookup option.
Default placeholder for the lookup search input.
Cancel button label on the form footer.
Validation message for an invalid email address.
labels.value: Please enter a valid email address.

labels.shortDescription: Warning toast for an invalid field ({0} = field, {1} = SObject).
labels.value: Field "{0}" is not valid for SObject "{1}"

labels.shortDescription: Warning toast when neither save mode nor update mode is selected.
labels.value: Save Mode or Update Mode must be selected to use this feature.

labels.shortDescription: Error toast when the form has no fields or FieldSet API Name.
labels.value: No fields or FieldSet API Name have been passed into this form.

labels.shortDescription: Field-level help for the phone input.
Success toast after a record is created.
Success toast after a record is updated.
Validation error for an empty required field.
Save button label in save mode.
Save button label in update mode.
Warning toast when update mode is used without a Record Id.
labels.value: You are attempting to update a record without providing a Record Id

labels.shortDescription: Fallback validation error when a custom validator gives no message.
Message of the error thrown after a failed Apex controller call.
Default title of an error toast.
Default title of an info toast.
Default title of a success toast.
Default title of a warning toast.
Warning title for an invalid rows-per-page value ({0} = the value).
labels.value: "{0}" is not a valid number for Display Rows per Page on this table.

labels.shortDescription: Warning body when the rows-per-page value is not a positive number.
labels.value: Please enter a valid number greater than 0.

labels.shortDescription: Next-page button label.
Pagination position text ({0} = current page, {1} = total pages).
Previous-page button label.
Default title of the flow Back button.
Default title of the flow Next button.
Default heading for the validation errors section.
Default heading for the validation warnings section.
Step popover row label for the Apex class name.
Step popover row label for the continue-on-error flag.
Empty state when a chain execution has no recorded steps.
Step status word: failed but the chain continued.
Step status word: not yet executed.
Card title of the step timeline.
Affirmative value in the step popover.
